name: Cedar Creek
subtitle: Cedar Creek · Cedar Creek
description: A popular Rainforest and Swimming Area, North of Brisbane. The Crag has alot of potential with a large number of problems and a variety of styles. The Crag is currently in the early stages of research and engagement with the council.

name: White Rock
subtitle: White Rock Conservation Park · Ipswich
description: White Rock is a large sandstone climbing area located within the White Rock Conservation Park, just south of Ipswich. The crag has alot of potential and features a wide variety of problems across multiple sectors. The park is popular for its natural beauty and recreational opportunities, and ACC is actively working with Ipswich City Council to establish formal climbing access and develop a guidebook for the area.

name: Tooheys Forest
subtitle: Nathan · Brisbane
description: Toohey Forest is a large bushland within South Brisbane. Boulders scattered throughout the forest providing alot of climbs within the shade of the trees. BCC has recognised the climbing potential of Toohey Forest and is supportive of ACC's efforts to establish a climbing area within the park. They have recently built a new staircase to the Main sector

name: Plunketts
subtitle: Yarrabilba · Logan
description: Plunketts is an emerging bouldering area in Logan's Yarrabilba region, featuring quality sandstone problems in a suburban setting. The crag has strong potential to provide accessible climbing opportunities for the growing local population. ACC is currently in the research phase for Plunketts, with access assessment and documentation identified as priorities.

name: Passchendaele
subtitle: Passchendaele Conservation Park · Passchendaele
description: Passchendaele is a large state forest located in Stanthorpe. The Crag is densely covered in granite boulders and has alot of potential for a large number of problems. The Crag is currently in the early stages of research and engagement with the council.
